frameworks.name: evidence accumulation / drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: history biases enter via accumulation drift and starting point

frameworks.name: choice history bias
frameworks.one_line: idiosyncratic repetition/alternation tendencies in random sequences

frameworks.name: cortical circuit decomposition (sensory/associative/motor)
frameworks.one_line: asks which cortical stage produces history-dependent behavior

core_question: Which cortical circuits generate idiosyncratic choice repetition and alternation biases during perceptual decisions?
key_assumptions: previous-trial signals can bias either drift or starting point
MEG band-limited activity indexes region-specific decision signals
sensory stimuli are independent across trials
manipulations: cortical region (parietal vs motor vs sensory)
key_findings: sustained gamma-band activity in posterior parietal cortex tracks the previous choice
sustained beta-band activity in motor cortex inversely reflects the previous motor action
parietal signal biases evidence accumulation toward repetition; motor signal biases starting point toward alternation
parietal but not motor signal mediates previous-to-current choice impact and tracks individual repetition bias
interpretations: persistent parietal activity is the cortical locus of perceptual choice repetition bias
motor and parietal history signals dissociate in both band and computational role
